revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:広島県立みよし風土記の丘]]
[[File:Hiroshima Prefectural History and Folklore Museum.JPG|thumb|240px|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill Museum (Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore)]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=right|frame-width=240|frame-latitude=34.785|frame-longitude=132.87}} {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima Prefecture|relief=1|caption=Miyoshi Fudoki Hill (Hiroshima)}}
'''Miyoshi Fudoki Hill''' (広島県立みよし風土記の丘) is a {{ill|Fudoki Hill|lt=Fudoki Hill|lt=Fudoki no Oka|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki no Oka|qq=}} located in Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City, Hiroshima Prefecture.<ref>https://dive-hiroshima.com/en/explore/1302/</ref><ref>https://www.hiroshima-bunka.jp/english/detail/086.html</ref><ref>https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/english-park.html</ref><ref>https://dive-hiroshima.com/en/explore/99/</ref><ref>https://en.my10.jp/spot/hiroshima-miyoshi-fudoki-hill.php</ref>
== Overview == The park opened in April 1979 (Shōwa 54).
The site includes the nationally designated {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Jōrakuji and Nanatsuduka Kofun Group" and the nationally designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} "Former Shinno Residence." Additionally, it features reconstructed ancient buildings, stone chambers, Sarcophaguss, a natural wildflower garden, and a bird forest.
== Facilities == * {{ill|Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja|広島県立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Former Shinno Residence - Important Cultural Property * {{ill|Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q55524556|lt=Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|1=Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster}} - National Historic Site * Reconstructed buildings from the Kofun period - {{ill|Pit-house|ja|竪穴建物|Pit-house=Pit-house|lt=Pit dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-house|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|1=Pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q940435}}, {{ill|Flatland dwellings|ja|平地建物|1=Flatland dwellings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flatland dwellings|qq=}}, {{ill|Raised-floor storehouses|ja|高床建物|1=Raised floor building|1=Raised floor building|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised-floor storehouses|13=User:Immanuelle/Raised floor building|qq=}} * Reconstructed stone chambers and Sarcophaguss: ** {{ill|Shinotsuhara Kofun Group|ja|篠津原3号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinotsuhara Kofun Group|1=Shinotsuhara Kofun No.3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65278009}} (horizontal stone chamber) ** {{ill|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|lt=Sakayatakatsuka Kofun|ja|酒屋高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakayatakatsuka Kofun|qq=}} (pit-style stone chamber) ** {{ill|Sōsuike Nishi Site|ja|宗祐池西遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsuike Nishi Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (box-type Sarcophagus) * Natural wildflower garden * Bird forest * Part of the Hiroshima Prefecture Nature Trail "Kiri no Sato Route."
<gallery widths="180px" heights="150px"> File:Jyoraku-ji and Nanatsuduka Kofun group.JPG|Jōrakuji and Nanatsuduka Kofun Group File:Nanatsuduka Kofun No.9.JPG|Nanatsuduka No. 9 Kofun File:Old residence of the Shinno family.JPG|Former Shinno Residence File:Tonomaruyama Tatara furnace.JPG|Relocated reconstruction of the Tonomaruyama Tatara Furnace<ref>Excavated remains of a late Kofun period tatara furnace in Dōkawa Town, Shōbara City, reconstructed here.</ref> </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * Former Shinno Residence (original location: Sera Town, Sera District, Hiroshima) (Building) - Designated on
January 26, 1980 (Shōwa 55).<ref>{{Cite Cultural Properties Database|102|3160|Former Shinno Residence (original location: Sera Town, Sera District, Hiroshima)}}</ref> * Excavated items from {{ill|Yadani Kofun|en|Yadani Kofun|qid=Q28688908|lt=Yadani Kofun|1=Yadani Kofun}} (Archaeological Materials) - Stored at Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore. Designated on June 28, 1994 (Heisei 6).<ref>{{Cite Cultural Properties Database|201|10115|Excavated items from Yatani Kofun}}</ref> {{hidden begin |title = Details of National Important Cultural Property "Excavated Items from Yatani Kofun" |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background: lightgrey; }} * Items from the main burial chamber: ** Beads: ** Jasper tubular beads (including fragments): 5 pieces ** Small glass beads: 3 pieces ** Iron tools: ** 1 iron chisel ** 2 fragments of iron knives * Items from the surrounding ditch: ** Special jar: 1 piece ** Special pedestal fragments: 2 pieces * (Accompanying designation) Pottery fragments: 1 set {{hidden end}} <gallery> File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|Special jar and pedestal artifacts from {{ill|Yadani Kofun|en|Yadani Kofun|qid=Q28688908|lt=Yadani Kofun|1=Yadani Kofun}} File:矢谷墳丘墓 出土品.JPG|burial goods excavated from Yatani Kofun </gallery>
=== Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * Fishing tools from the Gonokawa River Basin, including materials related to fishing grounds - Stored at Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore. Designated on December 21, 1999 (Heisei 11).<ref>{{Cite Cultural Properties Database|301|221|Fishing tools from the Gonokawa River Basin, including materials related to fishing grounds}}</ref>
=== National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q55524556|lt=Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster|1=Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster}} - Designated on October 12, 1972 (Shōwa 47); additional designation of site area on February 7, 1975 (Shōwa 50).<ref>{{Cite Cultural Properties Database|401|2342|Jōrakuji and Nanatsuduka Kofun Group}}</ref>
=== Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Important Cultural Properties (Tangible Cultural Properties): ** Excavated artifacts from the {{ill|Mibu Nishitani Site|ja|壬生西谷遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu Nishitani Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Archaeological Materials) - Partly stored at Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore. Designated on February 28, 1994 (Heisei 6).<ref>[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-202060120.html Excavated artifacts from the Mibu Nishitani Site] (Hiroshima Prefecture Education Board).</ref> ** Bronze bell with keshadashimondō design (excavated from Kurokawa Site) (Archaeological Materials) - Stored at Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore. Designated on December 4, 2017 (Heisei 29).<ref>[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-202060180.html Bronze bell with keshadashimondō design (excavated from Kurokawa Site)] (Hiroshima Prefecture Education Board).</ref> <gallery> File:壬生西谷遺跡出土 内行花文鏡.JPG|Jade-inlaid mirror excavated from Mibu Nishitani Site File:黒川遺跡出土 袈裟襷文銅鐸.JPG|Bronze bell with keshadashimondō design excavated from Kurokawa Site </gallery>
=== Others === <gallery> File:Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|Second burial chamber of {{ill|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|ja|酒屋高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} File:Shinotsuhara Kofun Group-3, sekishitsu.jpg|Relocated horizontal stone chamber from {{ill|Shinotsuhara Kofun Group|ja|篠津原3号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinotsuhara Kofun Group|qq=}} File:酒屋高塚古墳 出土品.JPG|burial goods from {{ill|Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|ja|酒屋高塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaya Takatsuka Kofun|qq=}} File:潮崎山古墳出土 三角縁神獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Reproduction of triangular-rimmed deity-beast mirror excavated from {{ill|Shiozaki Kofun|ja|潮崎山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiozakiyama Kofun|qq=}} File:寺町廃寺跡出土 鴟尾・三彩陶器.JPG|burial goods from {{ill|Teramachi_temple_ruins|en|Teramachi_temple_ruins|qid=Q55524856|lt=Teramachi temple ruins|1=Teramachi temple ruins}} File:横見廃寺跡 出土品.JPG|burial goods from {{ill|Yokomi_temple_ruins|en|Yokomi_temple_ruins|qid=Q11543464|lt=Yokomi temple ruins|1=Yokomi temple ruins}} File:安芸国分寺出土 軒丸瓦.JPG|Eaves tiles excavated from {{ill|Aki_Kokubunji|en|Aki_Kokubunji|qid=Q87504364|lt=Aki Kokubunji|1=Aki Kokubunji}} </gallery>
== Park Data == * Address: 122 Odakochi Town, Miyoshi City, Hiroshima Prefecture, 729-6216 * Park Area: 30 ha * Opening Hours: 9:00–17:00 * Parking: 130 spaces (Free) * Closed: Open year-round
{{japan-location-data|34|45|48|132|53|55|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|34.763333,132.898611|Miyoshi Fudoki Hill|nocoord=yes}}
== Access == * {{ill|Chūgoku Expressway|en|Chūgoku Expressway|qid=Q1091207|lt=Chūgoku Expressway|1=Chūgoku Expressway}} - 5 km from Miyoshi Interchange (IC) * 5-minute walk from the "Fudoki Hill Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Bihoku Kōtsū|ja|備北交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bihoku Kōtsū|1=Bihoku Corporation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387356}} line from {{ill|Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|en|Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|qid=Q6884517|lt=Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|1=Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)}} * Approximately 20-minute walk from {{ill|Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)|en|Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)|qid=Q11590345|lt=Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)|1=Kamisugi Station (Hiroshima)}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Chūgoku Region|ja|中国地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Chūgoku Region|1=list of Historic Sites in Chūgoku Region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363368}}
== External Links == * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/ Official Website of Miyoshi Fudoki Hill] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Cultural Properties Database]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{coord|34|45|48|N|132|53|55|E|region:JP-34|display=title}}
